methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Nano Nuclear Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B+

data.narrative:
Oklo is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $10B+ following $500M+ in total capital raised. The nuclear energy sector comprises a modest comp universe of 11 peers total—3 public (including Oklo itself) and 8 private entities spanning similar technology and development stages.

Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable to this valuation. The narrow peer set within nuclear energy—filtered by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints required for robust median analysis. While 7 same-sector peers exist in aggregate, the combination of Oklo's public status, capital base, and development stage produced insufficient overlap with other comparables meeting minimum statistical thresholds. A cross-sector median would introduce misleading benchmark bias given the specialized nature of nuclear technology and regulatory environment.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without applicable triangulation results. The $10B+ current valuation reflects market pricing in public markets but lacks quantitative peer support from the available comp universe. Key uncertainties center on the narrowness of the nuclear energy peer set, particularly the absence of mature public comparables at similar scale and stage. Resolution would require either expansion of the comp universe to adjacent energy technology sectors or collection of additional nuclear-specific transaction data. Further analysis should prioritize identifying transaction multiples or private-market valuations from the 8 private peers listed.
